commands.review_summary:
The branch enables the setup device-pair plugin when reachable, adds tray-side wizard console-log tailing, unwraps nested wizard note messages, auto-launches auth-looking URLs, and adds helper/tail tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows `LooksLikeOAuthUrl` accepts any `github.com` URL and generic `/oauth` or `/authorize` URLs, and the added tests explicitly accept a GitHub issue URL. I did not run the Windows onboarding flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human PR handling because contributor-owned real behavior proof is missing and the remaining blocker includes a security/product decision about automatic browser launching.

target.title: fix(wizard): surface upstream console output, unwrap nested notes, auto-launch OAuth URLs
target.body:
Fixes #640.

## Problem

Upstream openclaw plugins emit user-critical content (OAuth URLs, install fallbacks, error reasons) via `console.log` to gateway stdout rather than as `wizard.payload` WS frames. The tray UI only renders `wizard.payload`, so during several model-provider onboarding paths the user saw a blank/stuck step and the wizard either silently looped or bounced back to the provider list.

All wizard copy and step content is owned by upstream openclaw; this PR does not modify any of that text. It adds tray-side backstops so the missing content reaches the user.

## Changes

Three layered, narrowly-scoped fixes plus one already-committed gateway fix on this branch (`32a2c7a` — enables the bundled `device-pair` plugin so OAuth providers can actually pair after install).

**1. `WizardConsoleTail`** — `src/OpenClaw.SetupEngine.UI/WizardConsoleTail.cs`
Spawns `wsl.exe -d OpenClawGateway -- bash -c "tail -F /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-*.log"` and surfaces root-logger `console.log` lines into a new `ConsoleBanner` panel on the active wizard step. Filters out structured subsystem noise. Silently no-ops when wsl/distro is unavailable (remote/Tailscale case).

**2. `WizardPayloadHelpers.ExtractStepMessage`** — `src/OpenClaw.SetupEngine.UI/WizardPayloadHelpers.cs`
Reads `step.message` and tolerates the case where a plugin nests a `{type:note, title, message}` object instead of sending a string. Returns the string as-is, `"Title\n\nMessage"` for a nested note, or empty (never raw JSON). Shape-based, not provider-keyed.

**3. `WizardUrlLauncher`** — same file
Auto-opens OAuth/device-code URLs via `Windows.System.Launcher.LaunchUriAsync`. Conservative host allowlist (`auth.openai.com`, `auth.x.ai`, `accounts.google.com`, `login.anthropic.com`, `console.anthropic.com`, `claude.ai`, `github.com`, `auth.openclaw.ai`) plus pattern match on `/oauth`, `/authorize`, `response_type=code`, `device_code`. Per-session dedupe via `HashSet`, cleared in `StartWizardAsync` so **Start over** resets cleanly. Docs/security links are skipped.

**Plumbing** — `Pages/WizardPage.xaml{,.cs}`
New `ConsoleBanner` `StackPanel` (collapsed by default). `_consoleTail` + `_launchedUrls` fields reset on `StartWizardAsync`. `ApplyPayloadAsync` uses the new helper. `AppendLineTo` extracted as a shared renderer for body + banner; URL branch triggers auto-launch.

Helpers are XAML-free and linked into the tray test project via `<Compile Include>`.

## Tests

- **39 new xUnit tests** (15 tail filter, 24 helpers).
- Build: 5/5 projects green, win-arm64.
- Shared tests: 2045 pass, 0 fail.
- Tray tests: 968 pass, 0 fail.
- Manual validation across 6 onboarding paths: Codex browser, Codex npm-download, Claude error, xAI OAuth, Gemini OAuth, Start over.
